description_long: This object is a historical-style cannon mounted on a wooden carriage with four solid wheels. The cannon barrel is long, cylindrical, and made of dark metal, featuring decorative bands and a slightly flared muzzle. The carriage is constructed from dark wood, reinforced with metal brackets and bolts, and includes two pairs of wheels for mobility. Metal rings and fasteners are visible on the carriage, likely for maneuvering or securing the cannon. The overall design is robust, with the cannon barrel resting securely in a cradle atop the carriage. The object exudes a sense of age and durability, typical of artillery used in historical urban fortifications or military displays.
description: A long, dark metal cannon mounted on a sturdy wooden carriage with four wheels and metal reinforcements. The design is robust and historically styled, suitable for display or historical combat scenarios.
description_view_0: Front view showing the circular muzzle of the cannon and the symmetrical arrangement of the wheels and carriage.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the full length of the cannon barrel, the wooden carriage, and the four wheels.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back end of the cannon barrel and the structure of the carriage.
description_view_3: Opposite side view mirroring view 1, showing the length of the barrel and carriage details.
attribute_weapon_mounts: One fixed cannon barrel mounted centrally on the wooden carriage.
attribute_armor_plating: None visible; the carriage and barrel are exposed with no additional armor plating.
attribute_camouflage_pattern: None visible; the object is finished in solid dark metal and wood tones.
